claim: The digital identity programme enrolled 2 million users in 2022.
source: The agency's 2022 annual report records that the digital identity programme enrolled 2 million users during the year.
note: Source restates the claim with the same figures; supported.

claim: Open data releases increased by 40 per cent between 2019 and 2023.
source: Departmental figures show open data releases increased by 40 per cent between 2019 and 2023.
note: Source asserts the same magnitude and period; supported.

claim: The regulator issued 312 compliance notices in the 2021 financial year.
source: In the 2021 financial year the regulator issued 312 compliance notices, the highest on record.
note: Exact figure and year confirmed by source.

claim: AI procurement spending reached 500 million dollars in 2023.
source: Treasury data confirm AI procurement spending reached 500 million dollars in 2023.
note: Source confirms the spending figure and year.

claim: Citizen satisfaction with online services rose to 78 per cent in 2022.
source: The 2022 customer survey found citizen satisfaction with online services rose to 78 per cent.
note: Survey result matches the claim.

claim: The audit reviewed 47 agencies and found 11 non-compliant.
source: The audit reviewed 47 agencies and identified 11 as non-compliant with the data standard.
note: Both counts confirmed by source.

claim: Average call wait times dropped to 4 minutes in 2023.
source: Performance data for 2023 show average call wait times dropped to 4 minutes.
note: Source confirms the figure and year.

claim: The grant programme funded 1,200 small businesses in 2021.
source: Records show the grant programme funded 1,200 small businesses in 2021.
note: Exact count and year confirmed.

claim: Broadband coverage extended to 96 per cent of households by 2022.
source: The 2022 connectivity report states broadband coverage extended to 96 per cent of households.
note: Coverage figure confirmed by source.

claim: The pilot reduced average application processing from 21 days to 9 days.
source: Under the pilot, average application processing fell from 21 days to 9 days.
note: Source confirms the before and after figures.

claim: Section 12 of the Data Act 2019 requires annual transparency reporting.
source: Section 12 of the Data Act 2019 requires agencies to produce annual transparency reporting.
note: Statutory requirement confirmed by source.

claim: The department trained 8,500 staff in privacy awareness during 2023.
source: The department trained 8,500 staff in privacy awareness during 2023, per its workforce report.
note: Training figure and year confirmed.

claim: Cloud migration cut infrastructure costs by 23 per cent over three years.
source: Cloud migration cut infrastructure costs by 23 per cent over three years, the review found.
note: Cost reduction confirmed by source.

claim: The portal recorded 3 million unique visitors in the 2022 calendar year.
source: The portal recorded 3 million unique visitors in the 2022 calendar year.
note: Source restates the claim verbatim.

claim: Processing times fell by 18 per cent after the 2021 reform.
source: Processing times rose by 18 per cent after the 2021 reform, according to the performance dashboard.
note: Source states the opposite direction (rose, not fell); same magnitude and year.

claim: Open data releases grew by 30 per cent in 2022.
source: Open data releases sank by 30 per cent in 2022.
note: Source states a decline of the same magnitude; opposite of the claim.

claim: AI spending rose to 700 million dollars in 2023.
source: AI spending fell to 700 million dollars in 2023, down from a higher figure the prior year.
note: Source states a fall, not a rise; same end figure.

claim: Compliance rates rose to 92 per cent in 2022.
source: Compliance rates fell to 92 per cent in 2022, the lowest in five years.
note: Source states a fall to the same level; opposite direction.

claim: Average wait times fell to 7 minutes in 2023.
source: Average wait times rose to 7 minutes in 2023, up sharply on the prior year.
note: Source states a rise, not a fall; same end figure.

claim: The pilot enrolled 12,000 participants by 2021.
source: The pilot enrolled only 1,200 participants by 2021, far short of the 12,000 originally projected.
note: Actual enrolment was 1,200; 12,000 was an unmet projection.

claim: Broadband coverage rose to 95 per cent of households by 2022.
source: Broadband coverage fell to 95 per cent of households by 2022 after the network fault.
note: Source states a fall to the same level; opposite direction.

claim: Staff training hours grew to 40 per employee in 2023.
source: Staff training hours dropped to 40 per employee in 2023.
note: Source states a drop, not growth; same end figure.

claim: The agency closed 220 complaints in 2022.
source: The agency opened 220 complaints in 2022 but closed none of them that year.
note: Source states none were closed; contradicts the claim.

claim: Digital service uptake rose 25 per cent in 2021.
source: Digital service uptake fell 25 per cent in 2021 following the outage.
note: Source states a fall of the same magnitude; opposite direction.

claim: The reform saved 45 million dollars in 2022.
source: The 2022 reform produced no net saving and ran over budget, analysts concluded.
note: Source states there was no saving; contradicts the claim.

claim: Citizen trust increased after the transparency rules took effect.
source: Survey evidence shows citizen trust declined once the new rules began to apply.
note: Source states trust declined; opposite of the claim.

claim: The 2020 review recommended abolishing the licensing scheme.
source: The 2020 review explicitly recommended retaining and strengthening the licensing scheme.
note: Source recommends the opposite of abolition.

claim: Emissions from the public fleet rose 9 per cent by 2022.
source: Public fleet emissions were down by 2022 relative to the baseline.
note: Source states emissions fell; contradicts the claimed rise.

claim: The new system reduced errors to fewer than 100 per month.
source: After deployment the new system generated several thousand errors each month, more than before.
note: Source states errors increased; contradicts the claimed reduction.

claim: Audit coverage expanded to all 80 agencies in 2023.
source: Audit coverage in 2023 reached only a minority of agencies, well short of full coverage.
note: Source states partial coverage; contradicts the claim of all 80.

claim: The data programme cut fraud losses by 30 per cent in 2022.
source: The data programme published its 2022 governance charter and listed its steering committee members.
note: Source is about governance, not fraud losses; claim not addressed.

claim: Online lodgement reached 70 per cent of all tax filings in 2021.
source: The 2021 report describes the online lodgement portal's accessibility features and login process.
note: Source describes features, not the lodgement share; claim not addressed.

claim: AI ethics training was completed by 67 per cent of staff in 2023.
source: The 2023 AI ethics training module covers bias, transparency, and escalation pathways.
note: Source describes module content, not completion rates.

claim: The reform reduced average tribunal delays to under 30 days.
source: The reform package restructured the tribunal registry and introduced an online case tracker.
note: Source describes structural changes, not delay figures.

claim: Procurement from small businesses rose to 25 per cent in 2022.
source: The 2022 procurement policy sets sustainability criteria and supplier conduct expectations.
note: Source is about policy criteria, not the procurement share.

claim: The portal handled 2 million transactions in 2021.
source: The portal's 2021 maintenance window schedule and outage policy are documented here.
note: Source covers maintenance, not transaction volume.

claim: Customer satisfaction reached 4.5 out of 5 in the 2023 survey.
source: The 2023 survey methodology and sampling frame are described in the appendix.
note: Source describes method, not the satisfaction score.

claim: The agency resolved 95 per cent of FOI requests within statutory time.
source: The agency FOI disclosure log lists document categories released proactively.
note: Source is a disclosure log, not a timeliness statistic.

claim: Cloud adoption saved 15 million dollars across 2022 and 2023.
source: Cloud adoption guidance covers data residency, exit clauses, and shared-responsibility duties.
note: Source is guidance, not a savings figure.

claim: The training lifted data literacy scores by 20 points in 2021.
source: The 2021 data literacy curriculum lists its learning outcomes and assessment rubric.
note: Source describes curriculum, not score changes.

claim: The platform prevented 3,000 duplicate payments in 2022.
source: The platform's 2022 release notes describe the new search index and interface refresh.
note: Source covers release content, not payment prevention.

claim: Regional offices handled 18 per cent more cases after the merger.
source: Regional offices relocated to a shared building following the merger.
note: Source covers relocation, not caseload change.

claim: The framework launched in 2021.
note: Citation present but no source text supplied; cannot be checked.

claim: Spending rose 12 per cent according to the review (Smith, 2022).
note: APA citation present, source not retrievable here.

claim: Service uptake reached 60 per cent by 2023.
note: URL citation present, no source text supplied.

claim: The Act commenced on 1 July 2020.
note: Citation present, source not supplied; unverifiable offline.

claim: The review made 14 recommendations (Jones & Brown, 2021).
note: APA citation present, source not retrievable here.

claim: Costs were 450 million dollars over four years.
note: Citation present, no source text supplied.

claim: Trust scores improved in 2022 (OECD, 2022).
note: APA citation present, source not retrievable here.

claim: Coverage extended to 96 per cent of premises.
note: Citation present, no source text supplied.

claim: The pilot ran for 18 months from 2019.
note: Citation present, source not supplied; unverifiable offline.

claim: The reform reduced processing time by 18 per cent.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify out of band.

claim: Public sector AI spending reached 2 billion dollars by 2023.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.

claim: The framework was established under section 45 of the Public Sector Act.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.

claim: A 2019 review found algorithm-assisted decisions reduced appeals.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.

claim: Data matching recovered 780 million dollars in 2020.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.

claim: Workforce reductions averaged 12 per cent across four agencies.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.

claim: The 2020 census found 28 per cent lacked data literacy.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.

claim: Citizen trust declined without explanation requirements in 2023.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.

claim: Emissions from the public fleet fell 9 per cent by 2022.
note: No citation and no source; nothing to verify.
